channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Success Center Hosting Review Sessions Week 4/19-4/26
item.description: Week of 4/19- 4/26: Last Round of Exams! Finish strong with one of our Success Center Review Sessions: -Physics I with SASE: Sunday, 4/19 (5-7pm) at the Success Center -Diff Eq.: Sunday, 4/19 (3-5pm) at the Success Center -Embedded Control: Monday 4/20 (7-9pm) at the Success Center -Cal II: Monday, 4/20 (7-9pm) in the Nason Classroom -Physics II: Tuesday, 4/21 (7-9pm) in Nason Classroom -Data Mgmt: Wednesday, 4/22 in ECAV (8-10pm) (Championship Classroom)
item.author: Students

item.title: 2026 Commencement Program Preferred Name
item.description: If you would like your preferred name (instead of your legal name) to appear in the 2026 Commencement program, please send an email to graduation@rpi.edu with your preference with the subject line “2026 Commencement Program Preferred Name”.To ensure your request can be accommodated, you must notify the Registrar’s Office no later than Thursday, April 23, 2026.If the Registrar does not receive a response by this deadline, they will use your legal name in the program.
item.author: Students

item.title: We’re Hiring Our 2026-2027 Student Success Center Tutors
item.description: The Student Success Center is currently recruiting 2026-27 peer tutors across a variety of majors. This flexible, paid, on-campus role offers valuable experience in communication and leadership while supporting fellow students. Fall 2026 Success Center Applications are due May 15th: https://surveys.rpi.edu/jfe/form/SV_56fbplo7sUaJRgG
item.author: Students

item.title: Tutoring in Nason Classroom
item.description: Did you know we offer tutoring from our Success Center Tutors in Nason Hall? We offer Tutoring in the following courses in Nason: Physics I & II- Mondays, 7-9pm CAD - Mondays, 7-9pm CAL I & II -Tuesdays, 7-9pm CS I and Data Structures - Wednesdays, 7-9pm
item.author: Students

item.title: Math Colloquium: Mallory Gaspard from Princeton University
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 22, 2026 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM - This Mathematical Sciences Colloquium will feature a presentation titled “When is Camouflage Useful? A Case Study in Hover Fly Pursuit-Evasion Interactions” by Dr. Mallory Gaspard. She is a Postdoctoral Research Associate in the Leonard Lab and Levin Lab at Princeton University. She is broadly inspired by developing mathematical approaches to problems involving decision-making and optimal control under uncertainty, particularly those arising…
item.author: Community

item.title: CEE Seminar Series - Dr. Allison Godwin Cornell Duffield Engineering - Robert F. Smith School of Chemical & Biomolecular Eng.
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 22, 2026 1:00 PM - 2:00 PM - “Engineering Identity and Decision Making”. Engineering is often described as purely technical and objective, but engineering decisions are shaped by people—their experiences, identities, and assumptions about who engineers are and who engineering is for. In this seminar, I will introduce the core of my research agenda, engineering identity, or how individuals see themselves and how they believe others see them as engineers. Further details…
item.author: Community

item.title: Department of Chemistry Seminar Series
item.description: Date: Tuesday, April 21, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - This week’s seminar will be presented by Phillip E. Savage, Distinguished Professor of Chemical Engineering at Penn State. His topic will be “Hydrothermal Reactions of Biopolymers and Synthetic Polymers – Toward a more Circular Economy and Renewable Fuels”.
item.author: Community

item.title: IBM “Road to Practitioner” Quantum Program
item.description: IBM has announced its Road to Practitioner (R2P) program—a multi-week online quantum computing course beginning in May, taught by members of IBM’s quantum team. This is an excellent opportunity to learn about the current state of quantum computing and algorithms in a focused, instructor-led setting. Visit https://foci.rpi.edu/news-and-events for more details and the link to register.
item.author: Community

item.title: Connected Dreams: Advanced Digital Imaging Exhibition
item.description: You are cordially invited to Connected Dreams, an exhibition of our Advanced Digital Imaging class short study works in progress. The exhibition is in the Sage Labs Dean’s Gallery and extends to the Vertical Galleries (near the elevators on each floor) and sections of the Corridors of Creativity near Sage 2411. Please bring your mobile devices to experience the AR.
item.author: Community

item.title: Physics, Applied Physics & Astronomy Colloquium: Professor Gwo-Ching Wang, Department of Physics, Applied Physics and Astronomy, RPI
item.description: Date: Wednesday, April 22, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Title: Electron vision from fundamental matter wave scattering to practical metrology application
item.author: Community

item.title: Corpus Festival | April 23-25, 2026
item.description: A weekend of creative experiments and gatherings that attune us to how bodily forms and bodies in formation construct our world. How do we take part in society by physically showing up? We all play our part in shaping power, control, freedom, and mutual responsibility.
item.author: Community

item.title: Webinar-Friday April 24: Webex Training Session
item.description: Join us on Friday, April 24, from 11am-12pm for a hands-on Webex Training Session, part of the MMS Collaboration Software Training series. This session focuses on the Webex application and its integration with Outlook and other productivity tools, so you can efficiently schedule, launch, and manage meetings directly from the tools you use every day. Register now: https://rensselaer.webex.com/webappng/sites/rensselaer/webinar/webinarSeries/register/783d951c6ab8482e9198a7bd6075a700
item.author: Community

item.title: The Design Showcase Welcomes Guest Speaker, Brent Brunell ’94 from GE Vernova
item.description: Brent Brunell, ’94, the Technology Director of Digital & Electrical Systems at GE Vernova Advanced Research, will visit campus as a featured guest speaker for the Design Lab Showcase on Wednesday, April 22, 2026. Brent’s keynote presentation beginning at 3:00 PM in DCC 324.
item.author: Community

item.title: Design Lab Showcase to be held Wednesday, April 22nd, 2026 from 1:00 - 4:00 p.m.
item.description: The Design Showcase will be held on Wednesday, Wednesday, April 22nd, 2026 from 1:00 - 4:00 p.m. in the DCC Great Hall. Students from multiple engineering disciplines (Computer & Systems, Electrical, Industrial & Management, Materials and Mechanical Engineering) solve real-world design challenges for industry, entrepreneurs and academic/medical partners. Visit the showcase to: Interact with project teams and view their achievements. Learn how students solve complex design challenges. Get…
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Seminar: “Supervisory Essentials” June 10, 2026
item.description:
In this seminar, Managers and Supervisors will learn from each other and our Facilitators about important topics such as the Employee Value Proposition and Employee Experience, the six fundamental Leadership competencies at Rensselaer, and experience a “day in the life” of a Supervisor. In addition, the importance of managing employee performance, providing both rewards and recognition, employment law fundamentals and tools for building team capacity will be explored. For more information…

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
